title: Namespaces, Metadata, Labels, Selectors, Ownership, and Deletion · Mastery Check
summary: A release review compares fresh namespaces, metadata, labels, selectors, ownership, and deletion choices and accepts only answers tied to observable API, controller, workload, network, storage, or policy evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for resource identity and lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish namespaced and cluster-scoped resources, default namespaces, cross-namespace references, quotas, and isolation limits.
Design stable recommended labels and equality or set selectors without accidental overlap, mutable identity, or controller mismatch.
Separate identifying labels from non-identifying annotations and preserve controller, tooling, audit, and rollout metadata ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: K8s Namespace Scope Boundaries · changed case · Quiz K8s Namespace Scope Boundaries
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Distinguish namespaced and cluster-scoped resources, default namespaces, cross-namespace references, quotas, and isolation limits.
